provisions.description: Requires that during FY2024, any Executive order or Presidential memorandum issued or revoked shall be accompanied by a written OMB statement on budgetary impact including costs, benefits, and revenues. Statement must include a narrative summary, impact on mandatory and discretionary obligations/outlays by agency for 5 fiscal years, and revenue impact over 5 fiscal years. National emergency orders may have a 15-day delay. Threshold for Presidential memoranda is $100,000,000 regulatory cost.

provisions.description: Prohibits any department, agency, or instrumentality receiving appropriated funds from obligating or expending funds unless it has and administers a written policy ensuring drug-free workplaces.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 701. No department, agency, or instrumentality of the United States receiving appropriated funds under this or any other Act for fiscal year 2024 shall obligate or expend any such funds, unless such department, agency, or instrumentality has in place, and will continue to administer in good faith, a written policy designed to ensure that all of its workplaces are free from the illegal use, possession, or distribution of controlled substances
notes: Government-wide drug-free workplace requirement; applies to all agencies receiving FY2024 appropriated funds under this or any other Act

cross_references.target: Controlled Substances Act (21 U.S.C. 802)

provisions.description: Fixes maximum purchase price for passenger motor vehicles at $40,000 ($41,140 for station wagons) with exceptions for police-type vehicles (+$7,775), electric/hybrid demonstration vehicles (+5%), clean alternative fuel vehicles, and commercial alternative fuel vehicles.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 702. Unless otherwise specifically provided, the maximum amount allowable during the current fiscal year in accordance with section 1343(c) of title 31, United States Code, for the purchase of any passenger motor vehicle (exclusive of buses, ambulances, vans, law enforcement vehicles, protective vehicles, undercover surveillance vehicles, and police-type vehicles), is hereby fixed at $40,000 except station wagons for which the maximum shall be $41,140
notes: Station wagon maximum is $41,140
Police-type vehicle limit may be exceeded by up to $7,775
Electric/hybrid demonstration vehicles may exceed by up to 5%
Clean alternative fuel vehicle exception per P.L. 101-549
Commercial alternative fuel vehicles (electric, plug-in hybrid, hydrogen fuel cell) are fully exempt

cross_references.target: Electric and Hybrid Vehicle Research, Development, and Demonstration Act of 1976
Public Law 101-549

provisions.description: Makes appropriations available for travel expenses also available for quarters allowances and cost-of-living allowances in accordance with 5 U.S.C. 5922-5924.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 703. Appropriations of the executive departments and independent establishments for the current fiscal year available for expenses of travel, or for the expenses of the activity concerned, are hereby made available for quarters allowances and cost-of-living allowances, in accordance with 5 U.S.C. 5922–5924.
notes: Standard government-wide provision for overseas allowances

cross_references.description: Quarters and cost-of-living allowances for overseas employees

provisions.description: Prohibits payment of salary to any federal officer or employee in the continental United States unless they are a U.S. citizen, lawful permanent resident seeking citizenship, refugee/asylee who has filed for permanent residency, or a person owing allegiance to the U.S., with specified exceptions.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 704. Unless otherwise specified in law during the current fiscal year, no part of any appropriation contained in this or any other Act shall be used to pay the compensation of any officer or employee of the Government of the United States (including any agency the majority of the stock of which is owned by the Government of the United States) whose post of duty is in the continental United States unless such person
notes: False affidavit is a felony punishable by fine up to $4,000 or imprisonment up to 1 year

Exceptions: current employees as of date of enactment, international broadcasters employed by BBG, temporary translators, temporary field service (up to 60 days) during emergencies, and wildland firefighters (up to 120 days) employed by DOI or USDA Forest Service under international agreements

provisions.description: Makes appropriations for necessary expenses (including maintenance/operating) also available for payment to GSA for charges for space and services and renovation/alteration of buildings under applicable law.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 705. Appropriations available to any department or agency during the current fiscal year for necessary expenses, including maintenance or operating expenses, shall also be available for payment to the General Services Administration for charges for space and services and those expenses of renovation and alteration of buildings and facilities which constitute public improvements performed in accordance with the Public Buildings Act of 1959
notes: Standard government-wide provision

cross_references.target: Public Buildings Act of 1959 (73 Stat. 479)
Public Buildings Amendments of 1972 (86 Stat. 216)

provisions.description: Authorizes all federal agencies to receive and use funds from the sale of recycled materials and recovered federal records for recycling programs, environmental management programs, and other employee programs. Funds available until expended.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 706. In addition to funds provided in this or any other Act, all Federal agencies are authorized to receive and use funds resulting from the sale of materials, including Federal records disposed of pursuant to a records schedule recovered through recycling or waste prevention programs. Such funds shall be available until expended for the following purposes
notes: Funds available until expended for: (1) acquisition, waste reduction/prevention, and recycling programs per EO 14057; (2) environmental management programs including hazardous waste and pollution prevention; (3) other employee programs as authorized by law or agency head

cross_references.target: Executive Order No. 14057 (December 8, 2021)

provisions.description: Makes administrative expense funds for government corporations subject to chapter 91 of title 31 available for rent in DC, personal services contracts under 5 U.S.C. 3109, and related objects, with corresponding reduction if functions are transferred.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 707. Funds made available by this or any other Act for administrative expenses in the current fiscal year of the corporations and agencies subject to chapter 91 of title 31, United States Code, shall be available, in addition to objects for which such funds are otherwise available, for rent in the District of Columbia; services in accordance with 5 U.S.C. 3109
notes: Includes proviso that if functions budgeted as administrative expenses are transferred to other funds, administrative expense limitations are correspondingly reduced

cross_references.target: 31 U.S.C. Chapter 91

provisions.description: Prohibits use of any appropriation for interagency financing of boards, commissions, councils, committees, or similar groups (except Federal Executive Boards) that do not have prior and specific statutory approval to receive financial support from more than one agency.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 708. No part of any appropriation contained in this or any other Act shall be available for interagency financing of boards (except Federal Executive Boards), commissions, councils, committees, or similar groups (whether or not they are interagency entities) which do not have a prior and specific statutory approval to receive financial support from more than one agency or instrumentality.
notes: Federal Executive Boards are excepted from this prohibition

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds to implement, administer, or enforce any regulation that has been disapproved by joint resolution under the Congressional Review Act or other applicable law.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 709. None of the funds made available pursuant to the provisions of this or any other Act shall be used to implement, administer, or enforce any regulation which has been disapproved pursuant to a joint resolution duly adopted in accordance with the applicable law of the United States.
notes: Standard government-wide provision reinforcing Congressional Review Act disapprovals

provisions.description: Prohibits obligation or expenditure of more than $5,000 to furnish, redecorate, or purchase furniture for the office of any Presidential appointee or agency head unless advance notice is given to the Appropriations Committees.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 710. During the period in which the head of any department or agency, or any other officer or civilian employee of the Federal Government appointed by the President of the United States, holds office, no funds may be obligated or expended in excess of $5,000 to furnish or redecorate the office of such department head, agency head, officer, or employee, or to purchase furniture or make improvements for any such office, unless advance notice
notes: 'Office' includes entire suite of offices and any space primarily used by or directly controlled by the individual

provisions.description: Notwithstanding 31 U.S.C. 1346 and SEC. 708 of this Act, permits interagency funding for national security and emergency preparedness telecommunications initiatives benefiting multiple federal entities, as provided by Executive Order 13618.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 711. Notwithstanding 31 U.S.C. 1346, or section 708 of this Act, funds made available for the current fiscal year by this or any other Act shall be available for the interagency funding of national security and emergency preparedness telecommunications initiatives which benefit multiple Federal departments, agencies, or entities, as provided by Executive Order No. 13618 (July 6, 2012).

notes: Exceptions: serious flight risk, immediate threat, or medical determination

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds to destroy any document, recording, or record pertaining to death, potential sexual assault or abuse, or allegations of abuse, criminal activity, or disruption committed by an individual in DHS custody. Requires records be made available to charged/punished individuals upon request.

notes: Records must be made available to individuals charged, segregated, or punished, in accordance with applicable law

provisions.description: Applies the prohibition from section 519 of division F of P.L. 114-113 on funding for any position designated as a Principal Federal Official to any Federal funds, in the same manner as it applied to funds in that Act.

provisions.description: Requires the Under Secretary for Management of DHS to submit to Appropriations Committees a report on unfunded priorities for DHS and each component, for which discretionary funding would be classified as budget function 050, within 10 days of the President's budget submission. Report must include description, objectives, account information, and additional positions for each unfunded priority.
deadlines: 10 days after the President's budget submission
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) Not later than 10 days after the date on which the budget of the President for a fiscal year is submitted to Congress pursuant to section 1105(a) o
notes: Applies to budget function 050 (national defense) discretionary funding only
Defines 'unfunded priority' as requirements not funded in the budget that are necessary for operational/contingency plans

provisions.description: Requires Secretary of Homeland Security to notify congressional leadership and specified committees within 10 days of a presidential determination to provide protection for a covered individual (former/retired government official). Requires 15-day notice before extending protection and 30-day notice before terminating protection. Requires quarterly reports detailing each covered individual, scope, and cost of protection.
deadlines: 45 days after enactment and quarterly thereafter
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) Not later than 10 days after a determination is made by the President to evaluate and initiate protection under any authority for a former or retir
notes: Notifications may be classified if necessary
Must include threat assessment, scope, anticipated cost and duration

provisions.description: Prohibits DHS funds from this or any prior Act from being used to submit a project proposal to the Technology Modernization Fund unless the agency head concurrently notifies Appropriations Committees, submits a copy of the proposal, and provides analysis of how funding supplements or supplants existing requests. Prohibits obligation of TMF funds until 15 days after transmitting a detailed report to Appropriations Committees.

provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds provided to the Department of Homeland Security in this or any prior Act may be used by an agency to submit an initial project pr
notes: Applies to Technology Modernization Fund established by P.L. 115-91
Report must include full proposal, interagency agreement, repayment plan, and supplementation analysis
15-day congressional notification hold on obligation

cross_references.description: Technology Modernization Fund authorization in NDAA FY2018

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security, within 60 days of any FY2025 budget submission that assumes revenues or proposes reductions based on unenacted user fee proposals, to provide the Appropriations Committees with specific reductions in proposed discretionary budget authority commensurate with the assumed revenues in the event the fee proposals are not enacted prior to October 1, 2024.

notes: Contingent directive: only triggered if budget assumes unenacted user fee proposals

notes: Recurring rider prohibiting Arms Trade Treaty implementation absent Senate ratification

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to develop bimonthly estimates of noncitizen arrivals at the southwest border, broken out by demographics, independently validated, used in policy planning and budgeting, and shared with HHS, AG, State, and Appropriations Committees. Suspends reprogramming/transfer authority under SEC. 503 if estimates are not provided.

notes: Subsection (b) details required content: fiscal year coverage, demographic breakout, independent validation, budget integration

Subsection (d) suspends SEC. 503 reprogramming and transfer authority if estimates not provided

provisions.description: Amends Section 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 by applying a new subsection (s) exempting employees employed to adjust or evaluate disaster claims from overtime requirements for 2 years after a major disaster, provided they receive at least $591.00 per week. Effective on date of enactment.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: (a) Section 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 207) shall be applied as if the following text is part of such section:
notes: Creates FLSA overtime exemption for disaster claims adjusters for 2-year period after a major disaster
Minimum weekly compensation threshold of $591.00 or Secretary-established amount, whichever is greater
Defines 'major disaster' as any disaster or catastrophe declared by any State or Federal agency
Does not affect Section 13(a)(1) exemption
Effective on date of enactment per subsection (b)
Policy rider in an appropriations bill

cross_references.target: Section 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 207)
cross_references.description: Applies new subsection (s) exempting disaster claims adjusters from overtime

provisions.description: Provides flexibility for employers in the seafood industry with approved H-2B petitions to bring nonimmigrant workers into the U.S. at any time during a 120-day period from the start date. After 90 days, employers must complete a new local labor market assessment and offer the job to qualified U.S. workers. Exempts from DOL staggering rules.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: (a) Flexibility with respect to the crossing of H–2B nonimmigrants working in the seafood industry (1) In general Subject to paragraph (2), if a peti
notes: 120-day crossing window from petition start date for seafood industry H-2B workers
After 90 days: must list job orders in local newspapers on 2 Sundays, post on DOL Electronic Job Registry and at place of employment
Must offer job to equally or better qualified U.S. worker who applies and will be available
Exempts from staggering violation under 20 CFR 655.20(d)
H-2B nonimmigrants defined per INA section 101(a)(15)(H)(ii)(B)

provisions.description: Establishes that the prevailing wage for the H-2B program shall be the greater of the actual employer wage for similar employees or the prevailing wage for the occupational classification in the geographic area. Requires the Secretary to accept private wage surveys even when OES data are available unless methodology is not statistically supported.

provisions.raw_text: The determination of prevailing wage for the purposes of the H–2B program shall be the greater of—(1) the actual wage level paid by the employer to ot

Exception only where survey methodology and data are not statistically supported

provisions.description: Authorizes the Secretary of Labor to dispose of or divest real property on which the Treasure Island Job Corps Center and the Gary Job Corps Center are situated, by any means deemed appropriate including agreements or partnerships to construct a new Job Corps center. Net proceeds shall be available until expended for the Job Corps Program on Treasure Island and in and around San Marcos, Texas.

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds appropriated in this Act, including trust fund amounts, for any abortion or for health benefits coverage that includes coverage of abortion (Hyde Amendment).
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds appropriated in this Act, and none of the funds in any trust fund to which funds are appropriated in this Act, shall be expended
notes: Hyde Amendment: longstanding annual appropriations rider restricting federal funding of abortion
Subsection (a) prohibits funds for any abortion
Subsection (b) prohibits funds for health benefits coverage including abortion coverage
Subsection (c) defines 'health benefits coverage'

provisions.description: Provides exceptions to the Hyde Amendment abortion restriction for cases of rape, incest, or life-endangering physical conditions; clarifies that States may use non-Federal funds for abortion coverage; includes Weldon Amendment conscience protection prohibiting discrimination against health care entities that decline to provide, pay for, or refer for abortions.
provisions.policy_area: abortion funding exceptions and conscience protections
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) The limitations established in the preceding section shall not apply to an abortion— (1) if the pregnancy is the result of an act of rape or inces
notes: Subsection (a) provides rape/incest and life-of-mother exceptions to Hyde Amendment
Subsection (b) clarifies that State/local/private funds (excluding Medicaid matching) are not restricted
Subsection (c) preserves ability of managed care providers to offer abortion coverage with State funds
Subsection (d) is the Weldon Amendment: prohibits funds to any entity that discriminates against health care entities refusing to provide or refer for abortions
Subsection (d)(2) broadly defines 'health care entity' to include individual providers, hospitals, HMOs, insurance plans, etc.

cross_references.target: SEC. 506 of Division D
cross_references.description: Exceptions apply to the Hyde Amendment restrictions in SEC. 506

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds for creating human embryos for research purposes or for research in which human embryos are destroyed, discarded, or subjected to risk of injury or death beyond that allowed for fetal research under 45 CFR 46.204(b) and section 498(b) of the Public Health Service Act (Dickey-Wicker Amendment).

provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds made available in this Act may be used for— (1) the creation of a human embryo or embryos for research purposes; or (2) research

provisions.description: Restricts reprogramming of funds in this Act (and prior-year funds remaining available and fee-derived funds) without 15-day advance consultation and 10-day advance written notification to Appropriations Committees. Subsection (a) prohibits reprogramming that creates new programs, eliminates programs, increases funds for denied/restricted activities, relocates offices, reorganizes offices/programs, or contracts out Federal functions. Subsection (b) prohibits reprogramming in excess of $500,000 or 10 percent (whichever is less) that augments programs, reduces funding/personnel by 10 percent, or results from personnel savings changing approved programs.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds provided under this Act, or provided under previous appropriations Acts to the agencies funded by this Act that remain available
notes: Subsection (a) covers qualitative reprogramming restrictions (new programs, eliminations, relocations, etc.)
Subsection (b) covers quantitative reprogramming threshold of $500,000 or 10% whichever is less
Both require 15-day advance consultation and 10-day advance written notification to Appropriations Committees
Applies to current-year, prior-year, and fee-derived funds

provisions.description: Subsection (a) prohibits use of funds to request political affiliation, voting history, or positions on unrelated political issues from candidates for Federal scientific advisory committees. provisions.description: Funds available only to voluntary family planning projects offering a broad range of methods and services, meeting five requirements: (1) no quotas or numerical targets; (2) no payment of incentives, bribes, or financial reward for acceptors or program personnel; (3) no denial of rights or benefits for declining family planning; (4) comprehensible health information on benefits and risks; (5) experimental drugs/devices only in scientific studies with informed consent. USAID Administrator must report violations to Committees on Appropriations.

The President may waive for national security or humanitarian reasons, with publication in the Federal Register and 15-day prior notification to Appropriations Committees.
